title: Nested Headings Unnumbered: Hierarchy Test (No Numbering)
purpose: Diagnostic counterpart to nested-headings-deep. Same hierarchical structure (9 headings across 3 depth levels) but with all numbering suppressed (secnumdepth=-2). The visual font-size difference is the SOLE signal of nesting. A converter that flattens heading depth loses hierarchy entirely here because there is no numbering to recover from. Together with nested-headings-deep, this enables the precise finding: 'MinerU's flatten strategy is honest when source carries numbering, lossy when it does not.'
catalog_cells_exercised: Layer 1 / heading / depth-only signal (no numbering) / correctly_used
ground_truth_basis: source.tex (authored deterministically) + visual spot-check of source.pdf (verified 2026-04-22). Body text deliberately neutral fruit-encyclopedia content with no self-referential hierarchy hints — the LLM judge cannot answer hierarchy probes from body text leakage; it must read the markdown structure.

files.source.pdf: Rendered output — visual heading sizes only, no numbers
files.probes.json: 10 probes by construction, paired with nested-headings-deep
